---
title: "Week 3 — Hands-on bioinformatics I"
subtitle: "Code & data analysis with an AI in the loop, and capstone scoping"
---

## Topics

- AI-assisted scripting for bioinformatics.
- AI-assisted QC and EDA on a real dataset.
- Keeping ownership of scientific judgment.
- Capstone scoping: pick a question; draft a plan.

## Before class

Read:

- [Code assistance](../bioinformatics/code-assistance.qmd)
- [Data analysis](../bioinformatics/data-analysis.qmd)

Setup:

- Working R or Python environment, **pinned** (`renv` / `conda` / `uv`).
- Coding assistant set up and working in your editor.
- Course dataset downloaded (TBD — public, e.g., GEO).

## In class

- Pair programming session with AI on a small bioinformatics task.
- Walkthrough: QC pass on a small RNA-seq (or scRNA-seq) dataset — instructor narrates the discernment moves.
- Capstone brainstorming: each student leaves class with a one-paragraph capstone proposal and instructor feedback.

## After class

- **Mini-project** *(25%)*: a scoped AI-assisted bioinformatics analysis. Submit a reproducible Quarto/Jupyter report containing:
  1. **Baseline (AI-free)**: write a 30-line baseline analysis by hand. Run it. Save the output.
  2. **AI-assisted iteration**: iterate with an AI assistant. Save the working version.
  3. **Diff and discussion**: list every substantive change the AI suggested. For each, mark *accepted* / *rejected* and note why. The grade weights the *quality of your discernment* — accepting or rejecting both have to be defensible.
  4. **Reproducibility artifact**: pinned environment file (`renv.lock` / `environment.yml` / `uv.lock`) committed alongside the report.
  5. **Disclosure statement** per the [rubric](../course-syllabus.qmd#disclosure-rubric).

- **Capstone proposal** *(part of capstone, due end of week 3)*: one-page proposal for the week 4 capstone. Question, dataset/literature, planned approach, what AI will and will not help with.

## Supplementary

*TODO: dataset link, starter repo, baseline analysis stub.*
